purpose: AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace covering recipient-side upstream write-buf-zero command-message append execution.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel carrying the upstream write-buf-zero command-message token.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel, empty during this recipient write-buffer trace.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel, empty during this recipient write-buffer trace.

layers.summary: A write-buf-zero command-message token appends literal 0 to the recipient buffer and clears the active command source.
layers.boundary: This layer states recipient write-buffer command-message execution only; standard-signal and multi-command inputs remain rejection boundaries.

layers.summary: The recipient write-buffer node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete multi-cell command-routing circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one recipient write-buffer command-message activation.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0169 recipient write-buffer behavior and adds no standard-signal command semantics.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested recipient write-buffer transition.
